priority_mapping.0: P0 / This task should not be done in parallel with another.

assumptions: The first web PRD (prd_web.json) is fully delivered: Axum server, 9 coordinator API endpoints (health, status, run, stop, resume, dispatch, advance, reconcile, cleanup), SSE streaming, embedded asset serving, Dashboard page, Logs/SSE page, API client, Zustand store, useEventSource hook with reconnect/backoff.
The web server binds to 127.0.0.1:3450 by default with --host/--port/--assets CLI flags already available.
No authentication is required. The web client is accessible without login or token.
The Engine facade (macc-core) already exposes methods for config, plan, apply, worktrees, coordinator, doctor, and backups via the CLI/TUI. New API endpoints call these Engine methods.
The existing error system (ApiErrorEnvelope, ApiErrorBody, MACC-WEB-XXXX codes) and test infrastructure (WebTestEngine mock, tower::ServiceExt) are reusable for all new endpoints.
React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS 4 + Radix UI + Zustand + react-router-dom v7 are the established frontend stack.
MACC manages a single project (single repository). There is no multi-project management. The web UI always operates on the current project directory where `macc web` was launched.
All tasks target < 1 day of work for a single AI agent.

tasks.title: Modularize web.rs into sub-modules
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: cli/src/commands/web.rs is already ~2400 lines and will grow significantly with new endpoints. It must be split into domain modules before adding more code.
Key actions: Convert web.rs into a web/ directory module. Move existing code into: mod.rs (server setup, state, router assembly), errors.rs (ApiError types), coordinator.rs (existing coordinator handlers), sse.rs (SSE handler), assets.rs (embedded/dist serving). Preserve all existing tests in a tests/ sub-module or per-module test blocks.
Out of scope: Adding new endpoints or changing behavior. This is a pure structural refactor.
Success criteria: `cargo test` passes. All 35+ existing tests pass. The web server behaves identically. No public API changes.
tasks.objective: Enable parallel development of new backend endpoint modules without conflicts on a single file.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/ directory with mod.rs, errors.rs, coordinator.rs, sse.rs, assets.rs, and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/ directory.
Move server setup, WebState, router assembly into mod.rs.
Extract ApiError, ApiErrorEnvelope, ApiErrorBody, From<MaccError> into errors.rs.
Extract all coordinator_*_handler functions into coordinator.rs.
Extract SSE handler into sse.rs.
Extract embedded/dist asset serving into assets.rs.
Move or split tests into per-module test blocks.
Update cli/src/commands/mod.rs to use the new module path.
Run cargo test and verify all 35+ tests pass.
tasks.notes: This is P0 because it unblocks parallel work on all backend endpoint tasks. Each new domain (config, PRD, worktrees, etc.) will be a separate file in this module.

tasks.title: Define backend API types for all new endpoint domains
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: New API endpoints (config, PRD, worktrees, logs, doctor, backups, registry) need shared request/response types defined before implementation.
Key actions: Create cli/src/commands/web/types.rs with Serialize/Deserialize structs for all new API domains. Types must align with the spec (sections 7.3-7.18, 9.1-9.2) and existing Engine data structures.
Out of scope: Implementing handlers. Only type definitions and serde derives.
Success criteria: All new API request/response types compile. Types are documented with field-level comments. Types use the established error envelope pattern.
tasks.objective: Contracts-first: define all API shapes before implementing handlers.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/types.rs with all new API types.
steps: Define ApiConfigResponse (mirrors canonical config fields relevant to web UI).
Define ApiConfigUpdateRequest (partial update of config fields).
Define ApiPrdResponse (task list + metadata) and ApiPrdUpdateRequest.
Define ApiPlanRequest (scope, tools, flags) and ApiPlanResponse (ActionPlan: files, diffs, risks, consents).
Define ApiApplyRequest (scope, dry_run flag) and ApiApplyResponse (results, backup locations).
Define ApiWorktree (id, slug, branch, tool, status, path) and ApiWorktreeCreateRequest.
Define ApiRegistryTask (state, tool, attempts, heartbeat, delayed_until, events).
Define ApiRegistryTaskAction (requeue, reassign with tool/justification).
Define ApiLogFile (path, size, modified) and ApiLogContent (lines, total).
Define ApiDoctorReport (health_score, issues by severity) and ApiDoctorIssue.
Define ApiBackup (id, timestamp, files) and ApiRestoreRequest.
Add comprehensive doc comments for each type.
Run cargo check to verify compilation.
tasks.notes: Types should map closely to existing Engine structs (CoordinatorStatus, WorktreeInfo, DoctorReport, etc.) to minimize conversion boilerplate. Use #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] for JSON convention consistency with existing API.

tasks.title: Define frontend TypeScript types for all new API domains
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: Frontend pages need typed API client functions for new endpoints. Types must mirror the backend API types defined in WEB2-ARCH-002.
Key actions: Extend web/src/api/models.ts with TypeScript interfaces for all new domains. Extend web/src/api/client.ts with fetch wrappers for all new endpoints.
Out of scope: UI components or stores. Only types and client functions.
Success criteria: TypeScript compiles. All new client functions are typed and handle the standard error envelope. Functions are exported and ready for page consumption.
tasks.objective: Contracts-first: provide typed frontend API access before building pages.
tasks.result: Extended web/src/api/models.ts and web/src/api/client.ts covering all new API domains.
steps: Add TypeScript interfaces matching each backend API type: ConfigResponse, ConfigUpdateRequest, PrdResponse, PrdUpdateRequest, PlanRequest, PlanResponse, ApplyRequest, ApplyResponse, Worktree, WorktreeCreateRequest, RegistryTask, RegistryTaskAction, LogFile, LogContent, DoctorReport, DoctorIssue, Backup, RestoreRequest.

Add client functions: getConfig(), updateConfig(), getPrd(), updatePrd(), runPlan(), runApply(), getWorktrees(), createWorktree(), deleteWorktree(), runWorktree(), getRegistryTasks(), requeueTask(), reassignTask(), getLogs(), getLogContent(), getDoctorReport(), runDoctorFix(), getBackups(), restoreBackup().
All functions must use the existing error handling pattern (check response.ok, parse ApiError envelope on failure).
Run tsc --noEmit to verify type correctness.
tasks.notes: Keep types in sync with WEB2-ARCH-002 backend types. Use the same camelCase field names. The existing client.ts already has getHealth(), getStatus(), postCoordinatorAction() as patterns to follow.

tasks.title: Implement design system tokens and dark theme
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec requires a dark, console-grade theme (section 5.3) with consistent design tokens. The current UI uses default Tailwind light theme.
Key actions: Define CSS custom properties for the design system (colors, spacing, typography, shadows, radii). Implement a dark theme as the default. Add status semantic colors (severity color + icon + text label). Configure Tailwind to use these tokens.
Out of scope: Component implementation. Only tokens, theme, and base styles.
Success criteria: Dark theme is the default. CSS variables are defined in index.css. Tailwind config extends with custom tokens. A simple page renders with the dark theme.
tasks.objective: Establish the visual foundation for all UI components.
tasks.result: web/src/index.css with CSS custom properties. Updated tailwind.config.js with custom theme. Dark theme active by default.
steps: Define color palette CSS variables: --bg-primary, --bg-secondary, --bg-card, --text-primary, --text-secondary, --text-muted, --border, --accent, --success, --warning, --error, --info.
Define status semantic tokens: todo (neutral), active (blue), blocked (amber), merged (green), failed (red), paused (yellow).
Define typography tokens: --font-ui (sans-serif), --font-mono (monospace).
Define spacing and radius tokens consistent with spec section 5.3.
Update tailwind.config.js to extend theme with these custom properties.
Set dark background and light text as defaults on html/body.
Add a density toggle CSS class (comfortable/compact) that adjusts padding/gap.
tasks.notes: Spec requires: 'Default theme: dark, console-grade readability. Cards: consistent radius, subtle border, soft shadow. Typography: UI font for navigation, monospace for logs/paths/hashes.' Use Tailwind's darkMode: 'class' strategy.

tasks.title: Build shared component library
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec defines global components (section 5.5) reused across many pages: KPI Card, Issue Card, Stream Tile, Task List Item, Right Drawer Inspector, etc.
Key actions: Create reusable React components in web/src/components/ using Radix UI primitives + Tailwind styling + dark theme tokens.
Out of scope: Page-specific logic. Components must be generic and composable.
Success criteria: Each component renders correctly with the dark theme. Components accept props for data and callbacks. Components are accessible (ARIA labels, keyboard nav).
tasks.objective: Provide a library of reusable UI building blocks for all pages.
tasks.result: web/src/components/ with KpiCard, IssueCard, StreamTile, TaskListItem, RightDrawer, ConfirmDialog, StatusBadge, LoadingSpinner, EmptyState, ErrorBanner, Toast.
steps: Create KpiCard component: title, value, delta indicator, icon slot.
Create StatusBadge component: status text + color + icon (never color only per spec).
Create IssueCard component: severity, code, current/expected state, action buttons.
Create StreamTile component: title, tool badge, live log tail, status indicator, pause/copy actions.
Create TaskListItem component: task ID, title, state badge, tool, attempts, priority.
Create RightDrawer component: slide-in panel, pinnable, close on escape, overlay.
Create ConfirmDialog component: single confirm for Caution, double confirm/typed phrase for Dangerous actions.
Create Toast component: success/error/warning notifications with auto-dismiss.
Create ErrorBanner component: display API errors with retry/copy/open-logs buttons.
Create EmptyState and LoadingSpinner utility components.
Ensure all components use dark theme tokens and are keyboard accessible.
tasks.notes: Spec section 5.5 lists: KPI Card, Issue Card (Doctor), Stream Tile (Performer/Worker), Task List Item (Registry), Right Drawer Inspector, Command Palette, Notifications Drawer, Terminal Drawer. Command Palette and Terminal Drawer are separate tasks.

tasks.title: Build app shell with sidebar, top bar, and status strip
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec defines a global layout (section 5.1) with left sidebar navigation, top bar with project info/search/indicators, and bottom status strip. The current layout is a basic 200px gray sidebar.
Key actions: Replace the current Layout component with a full app shell matching the spec. Implement collapsible sidebar with two mode groups (Setup & Config, Ops). Add top bar and bottom status strip.
Out of scope: Command palette and search functionality (separate tasks). Status strip data fetching (use placeholders).
Success criteria: Sidebar shows all navigation items organized by mode. Top bar shows project path and connection indicator. Status strip shows placeholder system metrics. Sidebar is collapsible. Layout is responsive.
tasks.objective: Provide the application-wide navigation and status chrome.
tasks.result: Updated web/src/components/Layout.tsx with full app shell matching spec section 5.1.
steps: Replace current sidebar with two-section navigation: Setup & Config (/dashboard, /config/tools, /config/standards, /config/skills, /config/settings, /prd, /plan, /apply) and Ops (/ops/console, /ops/registry, /ops/live, /ops/locks, /ops/diagnostics, /ops/logs, /ops/backups).
Add collapse toggle to sidebar (icon-only mode when collapsed).
Add top bar with: project path display, connection status indicator (green/yellow/red dot), placeholder for search trigger (Ctrl+K).
Add bottom status strip with placeholders: coordinator state badge, active workers count, throttled tools summary.
Style everything with dark theme tokens.
Ensure sidebar navigation highlights the active route.
Add icons to each navigation item (use simple SVG or emoji placeholders).
tasks.notes: Spec section 5.1 layout adapted for single-project scope: Top bar shows the current project path (no project selector — MACC manages a single repository). Bottom status strip shows coordinator state, active workers, throttled tools. The status strip data will be wired in later tasks.

tasks.title: Set up route structure with lazy loading for all pages
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec defines ~20 routes (section 6). Currently only / and /logs exist. All new routes must be registered with lazy loading for performance.
Key actions: Update web/src/App.tsx with all routes from the spec. Use React.lazy() for code splitting. Create placeholder page components for routes that don't have implementations yet.
Out of scope: Implementing page content. Only route registration and placeholder components.
Success criteria: All routes from spec section 6 are navigable. Each route renders at least a page title. No 404 for any spec route. Bundle splitting works (each page is a separate chunk).
tasks.objective: Register all application routes so pages can be developed independently.
tasks.result: Updated web/src/App.tsx with all routes. Placeholder pages in web/src/pages/.
steps: Create placeholder page components for all new routes: Welcome, Init, Config/Tools, Config/Standards, Config/Skills, Config/Settings, Prd, Plan, Apply, Ops/Console, Ops/Registry, Ops/Live, Ops/Locks, Ops/Diagnostics, Ops/Logs, Ops/Backups.
Wrap each page import with React.lazy() for code splitting.
Add a React.Suspense boundary with a loading fallback in the layout.
Register all routes in App.tsx using react-router-dom v7 Routes/Route.
Add a catch-all route that redirects to /dashboard.
Verify navigation works for all routes via the sidebar.
tasks.notes: Keep placeholder pages minimal: just a heading with the page name. Actual implementations come in FE-* tasks. Route paths follow spec section 6: /welcome, /init, /dashboard, /config/tools, /config/standards, /config/skills, /config/settings, /prd, /plan, /apply, /ops/console, /ops/registry, /ops/live, /ops/locks, /ops/diagnostics, /ops/logs, /ops/backups, /help, /about.

tasks.title: Expand error codes from MACC-WEB-0000 to domain-specific codes
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: All web API errors currently use the placeholder code MACC-WEB-0000. The spec requires stable, specific error codes (section 9.2) for debugging and client-side handling.
Key actions: Define MACC-WEB-XXXX error codes per domain. Update the From<MaccError> impl to emit specific codes. Add retryable and recommended_action fields to the error envelope as required by the spec.
Out of scope: Changing error categories or HTTP status codes.
Success criteria: Each error category maps to a specific code range. The error envelope includes retryable and recommended_action fields. Existing tests still pass. At least 5 new tests validate specific error codes.
tasks.objective: Make errors predictable and actionable for the web client.
tasks.result: Updated error mapping with domain-specific codes. Extended error envelope. Tests for specific codes.
steps: Define error code ranges: MACC-WEB-1000 (validation), MACC-WEB-2000 (not found), MACC-WEB-3000 (conflict), MACC-WEB-4000 (dependency/engine), MACC-WEB-5000 (internal).
Add retryable: bool and recommended_action: Option<String> fields to ApiErrorBody.
Update From<MaccError> to map each MaccError variant to a specific code and set retryable/recommended_action.
Update docs/ERRORS.md with the new code catalog.
Add integration tests asserting specific error codes for known failure scenarios.
Run cargo test to verify all existing tests still pass.
tasks.notes: Spec section 9.2: 'code (stable string), message (operator readable), retryable (bool), recommended_action (string)'. The existing ApiErrorBody has code, category, message, context, cause. Add retryable and recommended_action.

tasks.title: Implement config read/write API endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The web UI needs to read and edit .macc/macc.yaml (spec sections 7.4-7.5, 9.1). Currently no config endpoints exist.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/config and PUT /api/v1/config handlers in a new config.rs module. GET returns the parsed canonical config as JSON. PUT validates input, creates a backup, writes the updated config.
Out of scope: Schema-driven form generation. Just read/write JSON.
Success criteria: GET returns the current config. PUT validates and writes with backup. Both return standard error envelopes on failure. At least 4 integration tests (read, write success, validation error, backup creation).
tasks.objective: Enable web-based config viewing and editing.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/config.rs with GET and PUT /api/v1/config handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/config.rs.
Implement get_config_handler: call Engine to read canonical config, serialize to JSON.
Implement update_config_handler: parse request body, validate, call Engine to write config (which handles backup), return success.
Map errors to the standard ApiError types.
Register routes in the main router (mod.rs).
Add integration tests: GET success, PUT success, PUT with invalid data, verify backup created.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Engine already handles config read/write for CLI/TUI. The handler should call Engine methods, not parse files directly. Config validation should use the same rules as `macc plan`.

tasks.title: Implement PRD read/write API endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The PRD Editor (spec section 7.7) needs API access to read and write prd.json files.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/prd and PUT /api/v1/prd handlers. GET reads and parses the project PRD. PUT validates the JSON structure, creates a backup, and writes. Support an optional ?path= query param for worktree-specific PRDs.
Out of scope: PRD validation beyond JSON schema. Graph/dependency analysis.
Success criteria: GET returns parsed PRD as JSON. PUT validates, backs up, and writes. Path parameter selects project vs worktree PRD. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based PRD viewing and editing.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/prd.rs with GET and PUT /api/v1/prd handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/prd.rs.
Implement get_prd_handler: read PRD file, parse JSON, return typed response with tasks and metadata.
Implement update_prd_handler: parse request body, validate JSON structure (valid task IDs, required fields), backup existing file, write new content.
Support optional query param ?path=<worktree_path> to read/write worktree-specific PRDs.
Map file-not-found and parse errors to standard ApiError.
Register routes in the main router.
Add integration tests: read project PRD, read worktree PRD, write with backup, validation error.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: PRD files are plain JSON. Engine may or may not have a PRD read method — if not, the handler can read the file directly from the project paths (ProjectPaths.root / prd.json). Backup should go to .macc/backups/.

tasks.title: Implement worktree CRUD API endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Worktrees page (spec section 7.10) needs endpoints to list, create, and delete worktrees.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/worktrees, POST /api/v1/worktrees, and DELETE /api/v1/worktrees/{id} handlers. List returns all worktrees with status. Create accepts slug/tool/count/base params. Delete requires confirmation (double-confirm for force delete).
Out of scope: Worktree run/logs (separate task). Bulk operations.
Success criteria: GET returns worktree list with status, branch, tool, path. POST creates a worktree via Engine. DELETE removes with optional force flag. At least 6 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based worktree management.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/worktrees.rs with CRUD handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/worktrees.rs.
Implement list_worktrees_handler: call Engine to list worktrees, map to ApiWorktree response.
Implement create_worktree_handler: parse request body (slug, tool, count, base_branch, scope), call Engine worktree create, return created worktree info.
Implement delete_worktree_handler: accept path param ID + optional force/remove_branch query params, call Engine worktree remove, return success.
Map errors (worktree not found, creation failure, removal blocked) to standard ApiError.
Register routes: GET /api/v1/worktrees, POST /api/v1/worktrees, DELETE /api/v1/worktrees/:id.
Add integration tests: list empty, list with worktrees, create, create with invalid params, delete, delete non-existent.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Engine already has worktree management methods (used by `macc worktree create/remove/list`). The handler should call these methods, not implement worktree logic directly.

tasks.title: Implement registry task list and action endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Registry & Task Inspector (spec section 7.12) needs API access to view task states and perform operator actions (requeue, reassign).
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/registry/tasks and POST /api/v1/registry/tasks/{id}/{action} handlers. List returns all tasks with state, tool, attempts, heartbeat. Actions support requeue and reassign.
Out of scope: Task event history timeline (requires separate event query). Bulk actions.
Success criteria: GET returns all registry tasks with full metadata. POST requeue resets a task to todo. POST reassign changes the tool assignment. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based registry inspection and task management.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/registry.rs with list and action handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/registry.rs.

Implement list_registry_tasks_handler: read task registry from Engine/storage, map each task to ApiRegistryTask (id, title, state, tool, attempts, last_heartbeat, delayed_until, error_code, priority).
Implement task_action_handler: accept task ID and action (requeue/reassign) from path, parse optional body (tool for reassign, justification note), call Engine/storage to update task state.
Map errors (task not found, invalid state transition, invalid action) to standard ApiError.
Register routes: GET /api/v1/registry/tasks, POST /api/v1/registry/tasks/:id/:action.
Add integration tests: list tasks, requeue a failed task, reassign a task, invalid action.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: The coordinator uses a SQLite-backed registry. The handler should use coordinator_storage or Engine methods to access it. Requeue should transition a failed/blocked task back to todo. Reassign should update the task's tool assignment.

tasks.title: Implement plan API endpoint
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Plan page (spec section 7.8) needs an endpoint to run plan and return the ActionPlan with diffs.
Key actions: Add POST /api/v1/plan handler. Accepts scope (tools, worktrees), offline flag. Returns the ActionPlan: file list, diffs, risk levels, consent requirements.
Out of scope: Apply. Only plan preview.
Success criteria: POST returns a structured ActionPlan. Diffs are included as strings. Risk levels are classified. At least 3 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based plan preview.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/plan.rs with POST /api/v1/plan handler and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/plan.rs.
Implement run_plan_handler: parse request body (optional tools list, offline flag), call Engine::plan(), serialize ActionPlan to JSON response.
Include in response: files to write (path, content preview, risk level), diffs (unified diff format), consent requirements (safe/caution/dangerous classification).
Map errors (config not found, engine error) to standard ApiError.
Register route: POST /api/v1/plan.
Add integration tests: plan with defaults, plan with specific tools, plan with no config.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Engine already has a plan() method used by `macc plan`. The web handler wraps it with JSON serialization. The plan result may reference ActionPlan from core — map it to the API types defined in WEB2-ARCH-002.

tasks.title: Implement apply API endpoint
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Apply page (spec section 7.9) needs an endpoint to execute apply with safety gates.
Key actions: Add POST /api/v1/apply handler. Accepts scope, dry_run flag. Returns results summary, backup locations. Requires explicit confirmation in request body.
Out of scope: User-scope writes (require separate --allow-user-scope handling).
Success criteria: POST with dry_run returns preview without writing. POST with confirmed=true writes and returns results + backup location. Refuses if confirmed is false or missing. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based config apply with safety gates.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/apply.rs with POST /api/v1/apply handler and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/apply.rs.
Implement run_apply_handler: parse request body (scope, dry_run, confirmed boolean), call Engine::apply() or Engine::plan() for dry_run.
If dry_run: return the plan preview (same as /api/v1/plan response).
If not confirmed: return error MACC-WEB-1001 'Confirmation required'.
If confirmed: call Engine::apply(), return results (files written, backup location, warnings).
Map errors to standard ApiError.
Register route: POST /api/v1/apply.
Add integration tests: dry run, apply without confirmation (should fail), apply with confirmation, apply error.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Apply is a Caution-level action per spec section 4.2. The confirmed flag in the request body serves as the consent gate. The frontend will show a ConfirmDialog before sending confirmed=true.

tasks.title: Implement worktree run and per-worktree log stream endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Worktrees page and Worker Detail (spec sections 7.10, 7.14) need endpoints to run a performer in a worktree and stream its logs.
Key actions: Add POST /api/v1/worktrees/{id}/run and GET /api/v1/worktrees/{id}/logs (SSE) handlers.
Out of scope: Terminal access (separate task). Full worker detail data.
Success criteria: POST run triggers a performer run in the specified worktree. GET logs streams performer log lines via SSE. Run returns immediately with status. At least 3 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-triggered worktree execution and live log viewing.
tasks.result: Handlers added to cli/src/commands/web/worktrees.rs for run and logs streaming.
steps: Add run_worktree_handler: accept worktree ID path param, call Engine worktree run, return success with run status.
Add worktree_logs_handler: accept worktree ID, open performer log file for the worktree, stream new lines via SSE (using tokio::fs and inotify or polling).
Include SSE event envelope: id (line number), event type (log_line), data (JSON with timestamp, level, message).
Add heartbeat events every 15s to keep the connection alive.
Map errors (worktree not found, already running, log file missing) to standard ApiError.
Register routes: POST /api/v1/worktrees/:id/run, GET /api/v1/worktrees/:id/logs.
Add integration tests: run success, run non-existent worktree, logs stream format.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Performer logs are in .macc/log/performer/<worktree_slug>/. The SSE handler should tail the latest log file. For the run handler, Engine already has worktree run methods used by `macc worktree run`.

tasks.title: Implement logs list and read endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Logs Explorer (spec section 7.17) needs endpoints to browse and read log files from .macc/log/.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/logs (list log files by category) and GET /api/v1/logs/{path} (read content with pagination). Support search within files.
Out of scope: SSE tail streaming (separate task). Timeline visualization.
Success criteria: GET list returns categorized log files (coordinator, performer) with size and date. GET read returns paginated content. Search query filters lines. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based log browsing and reading.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/logs.rs with list and read handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/logs.rs.
Implement list_logs_handler: scan .macc/log/ directory tree, return categorized list of log files (path, size, modified timestamp, category: coordinator/performer).
Implement read_log_handler: accept path param (URL-encoded relative path under .macc/log/), read file with optional query params: offset (line number), limit (max lines), search (filter pattern).
Sanitize path to prevent directory traversal (must stay under .macc/log/).
Return ApiLogContent with lines array, total line count, and whether more lines exist.
Map errors (file not found, path traversal attempt) to standard ApiError.
Register routes: GET /api/v1/logs, GET /api/v1/logs/*path.
Add integration tests: list logs, read file, read with pagination, read with search, path traversal rejection.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: CRITICAL: The path parameter must be sanitized to prevent reading arbitrary files. Only allow paths under .macc/log/. Reject any path containing '..' or absolute paths.

tasks.title: Implement log tail SSE stream endpoint
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec requires live log tailing via SSE (section 7.17, 8.1). Multiple concurrent tail streams must be supported for the Live Wall.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/logs/tail?path=... SSE endpoint that streams new lines as they're appended to a log file.
Out of scope: Terminal sessions. Full structured event parsing.
Success criteria: SSE stream emits new log lines within 1s of being written. Supports Last-Event-ID for reconnect. Heartbeats every 15s. Handles file rotation gracefully. At least 3 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable real-time log tailing for the Live Wall and Logs Explorer.
tasks.result: SSE tail handler added to cli/src/commands/web/logs.rs.
steps: Add tail_log_handler: accept ?path= query param, validate path (same sanitization as read).
Open the log file and seek to end (or to a position based on Last-Event-ID header).
Stream new lines via SSE using tokio file watching (poll every 500ms or use notify crate).
Each SSE event: id (byte offset or line number), event type (log_line), data (JSON: timestamp, content).
Send heartbeat events every 15s.
Handle file truncation/rotation: detect size decrease and re-read from beginning.
Limit concurrent tail streams per client (max 25 for Live Wall support).
Register route: GET /api/v1/logs/tail.
Add integration tests: stream format, heartbeat, reconnect with Last-Event-ID.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: The existing /api/v1/events SSE endpoint for coordinator events is a good pattern to follow. This endpoint is for arbitrary log files. The spec mentions 20+ concurrent stream tiles (section 10), so this must handle high fan-out efficiently.

tasks.title: Implement doctor diagnostic endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Diagnostics page (spec section 7.16) needs endpoints to run doctor checks and apply safe fixes.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/doctor (run diagnostics, return report) and POST /api/v1/doctor/fix (apply safe fixes with backup).
Out of scope: Custom fix logic. Doctor already has --fix capability.
Success criteria: GET returns structured report with health score, issues by severity, categories. POST fix applies safe fixes and returns results. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based system diagnostics and safe auto-fix.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/doctor.rs with GET and POST handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/doctor.rs.

Implement get_doctor_handler: call Engine::doctor() or equivalent, map DoctorReport to ApiDoctorReport (health_score, issues array with severity, code, description, current_state, expected_state, fix_available).
Implement run_doctor_fix_handler: parse optional request body (specific issue codes to fix, or all safe fixes), call Engine::doctor_fix(), return results (fixed count, failed count, backup location).
Map errors to standard ApiError.
Register routes: GET /api/v1/doctor, POST /api/v1/doctor/fix.
Add integration tests: report with no issues, report with issues, fix success, fix partial failure.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Engine already has doctor functionality used by `macc doctor --fix`. The web handler wraps it with JSON serialization. Doctor fix is a Caution-level action per spec section 4.2 — the frontend should show confirmation before calling POST.

tasks.title: Implement backup list and restore endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Backups & Restore page (spec section 7.18) needs endpoints to list backups and restore from them.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/backups (list by timestamp) and POST /api/v1/backups/{id}/restore (restore with confirmation).
Out of scope: Diff computation between backup and current (can be added later).
Success criteria: GET returns chronological backup list with file counts and timestamps. POST restore requires confirmed=true and creates a 'restore backup' before restoring. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based backup browsing and restoration.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/backups.rs with list and restore handlers and tests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/backups.rs.
Implement list_backups_handler: scan .macc/backups/ directory, return list of ApiBackup (id/timestamp, file list with paths and sizes, total size).
Implement restore_backup_handler: accept backup ID path param + confirmed boolean in body, validate backup exists, create 'pre-restore' backup of current state, restore files, return results.
If not confirmed: return error MACC-WEB-1001 'Confirmation required'.
Map errors (backup not found, restore failure) to standard ApiError.
Register routes: GET /api/v1/backups, POST /api/v1/backups/:id/restore.
Add integration tests: list empty, list with backups, restore with confirmation, restore without confirmation.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Restore is a Dangerous-level action per spec section 4.2. The frontend should show a double-confirm dialog. Engine already has restore functionality used by `macc restore`.

tasks.title: Add ops audit logging middleware
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec requires all operator actions to be logged to .macc/log/ops.jsonl (section 4.6) for traceability.
Key actions: Add an Axum middleware layer that logs all mutating API requests (POST, PUT, DELETE) to ops.jsonl with structured data.
Out of scope: Log rotation. Read-only requests are not logged.
Success criteria: Every POST/PUT/DELETE request appends a structured JSON line to ops.jsonl with: timestamp, action (method + path), inputs summary, result (status code), duration. At least 3 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Provide an audit trail for all operator actions via the web UI.
tasks.result: Axum middleware in cli/src/commands/web/audit.rs that logs mutating requests.
steps: Create cli/src/commands/web/audit.rs.
Implement an Axum middleware (tower Layer) that intercepts POST/PUT/DELETE requests.
After the handler completes, append a JSON line to .macc/log/ops.jsonl with: iso8601 timestamp, method, path, status code, duration_ms, optional request summary.
Use tokio::fs for async file append.
Skip logging for SSE endpoints and health checks.
Register the middleware layer in the router (mod.rs).
Add integration tests: verify ops.jsonl entry after a POST request, verify no entry for GET, verify format.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Spec section 4.6: 'who/what/when (local user), action, inputs, result summary, links to logs.' Since there's no auth, 'who' is the local system user. Keep the logging lightweight to avoid request latency.

tasks.title: Implement tool cooldown management API endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The coordinator automatically throttles tools on E602 (quota exhaustion) with configurable cooldown durations persisted in SQLite. Operators need API endpoints to view, manually set, and clear tool cooldowns — mirroring the CLI commands `macc coordinator tool-cooldown-list/set/clear` that already exist.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/coordinator/tool-cooldown (list all), POST /api/v1/coordinator/tool-cooldown (set cooldown), DELETE /api/v1/coordinator/tool-cooldown/:tool (clear cooldown). All endpoints delegate to the existing core functions in coordinator_workflow.rs.
Out of scope: Automatic cooldown policy configuration (handled by macc.toml). Only manual override and visibility.
Success criteria: GET returns an array of ToolCooldownEntry with tool_id, throttled_until (epoch), remaining_seconds, backoff_seconds. POST accepts {tool, duration_seconds} and creates/updates a cooldown. DELETE removes a cooldown. Response uses the standard API envelope. At least 3 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Expose tool cooldown management to the web UI, matching CLI capabilities.
tasks.result: New endpoints in cli/src/commands/web/coordinator.rs (or a new cooldown.rs sub-module) with tests.
steps: Define ApiToolCooldownEntry response type (already exists in coordinator.rs as a Serialize struct — reuse it).
Define ApiToolCooldownSetRequest { tool: String, duration_seconds: u64 }.
Implement get_tool_cooldowns_handler: call SqliteStorage::load_throttle_registry(), compute remaining_seconds, return sorted list.
Implement set_tool_cooldown_handler: parse request body, call SqliteStorage::upsert_tool_throttle(), return updated list.
Implement clear_tool_cooldown_handler: extract tool from path, call SqliteStorage::delete_tool_throttle(), return updated list.
Register routes: GET /api/v1/coordinator/tool-cooldown, POST /api/v1/coordinator/tool-cooldown, DELETE /api/v1/coordinator/tool-cooldown/:tool.
Add integration tests: list empty, set a cooldown and verify list, clear and verify removed.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: The core logic already exists: SqliteStorage has load_throttle_registry(), upsert_tool_throttle(), delete_tool_throttle(). The CLI commands tool-cooldown-list/set/clear in coordinator_workflow.rs are the reference implementation. The web endpoints are thin wrappers around the same SQLite functions. The coordinator's runtime throttle_registry (in-memory) is loaded from SQLite on startup, so manual changes via the API will take effect on the next coordinator cycle.

tasks.title: Build Tool Cooldown Manager panel in Coordinator Console
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The Coordinator Console (WEB2-FE-CONSOLE-001) already shows throttled tools as chips in the header, but operators need to view detailed cooldown state and manually set/clear cooldowns. This is especially important when a tool hits E602 quota exhaustion — the operator may want to extend or clear the cooldown.
Key actions: Add a Tool Cooldown Manager panel to the Console page (or as a collapsible section in the Resources panel). Show all active cooldowns with countdown timers and provide set/clear actions.
Out of scope: Cooldown policy editing (rate_limit settings in config page). Only manual override.
Success criteria: Panel shows a table of active tool cooldowns: tool name, remaining time (live countdown), throttled_until timestamp, backoff duration. Each row has a Clear button. A Set Cooldown form allows entering tool name and duration. Set/Clear actions call the API and refresh the list. Empty state shows 'No active cooldowns'.
tasks.objective: Give operators visibility and manual control over tool cooldown state from the web UI.
tasks.result: ToolCooldownPanel component integrated into the Coordinator Console page.
steps: Add TypeScript interface ToolCooldownEntry { tool_id: string; throttled_until: number; remaining_seconds: number; backoff_seconds: number } to web/src/api/models.ts.
Add API client functions: getToolCooldowns(), setToolCooldown(tool, durationSeconds), clearToolCooldown(tool) to web/src/api/client.ts.
Create web/src/components/ToolCooldownPanel.tsx.
Implement cooldown table: columns for Tool, Remaining (live countdown using setInterval), Throttled Until (formatted timestamp), Backoff, Actions (Clear button).
Implement Set Cooldown form: tool name input (with dropdown of known tools from config), duration input (seconds, with presets for 1h/2h/6h/12h/24h), Set button.
Wire Clear button to clearToolCooldown() API, refresh list on success.
Wire Set button to setToolCooldown() API, refresh list on success.
Show empty state when no cooldowns are active.
Integrate panel into Console.tsx as a section in the Resources panel area.
Auto-refresh cooldown list every 30s.
tasks.notes: The backend API endpoints are defined in WEB2-BE-COOL-001. The panel should match the dark theme and design tokens. The countdown timer should tick down in real-time (update remaining_seconds locally between API polls). Tool names can be populated from the config API (tools.enabled list). This is high priority since E602 quota exhaustion events are common during MACC coordinator runs and operators need immediate visibility.

tasks.title: Implement terminal WebSocket gateway
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec requires embedded terminals (section 7.19) with PTY over WebSocket for project root and per-worktree shells.
Key actions: Add POST /api/v1/terminal (create session) and WS /api/v1/terminal/{session} (attach to PTY). Implement PTY spawning with directory restriction.
Out of scope: Session persistence across server restarts. Multiple simultaneous sessions per terminal.
Success criteria: POST creates a terminal session bound to project root or a worktree path. WebSocket connection streams PTY I/O bidirectionally. Session terminates on disconnect or idle timeout. Directory restriction is enforced. At least 3 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Enable embedded terminal access from the web UI.
tasks.result: cli/src/commands/web/terminal.rs with session management and WebSocket PTY streaming.
steps: Add portable-pty or similar PTY crate to cli/Cargo.toml.
Create cli/src/commands/web/terminal.rs.
Implement create_terminal_handler: accept body with terminal type (project/worktree) and optional worktree ID, validate path, spawn PTY with shell (bash) in the target directory, return session ID.
Implement terminal_ws_handler: accept WebSocket upgrade, lookup session by ID, bridge WebSocket frames to PTY stdin/stdout bidirectionally.
Enforce directory restriction: PTY working directory must be project root or an existing worktree path.
Add idle timeout (5 minutes default): kill PTY if no I/O for the timeout period.
Add session cleanup on WebSocket disconnect.
Limit concurrent sessions (max 5).
Register routes: POST /api/v1/terminal, GET /api/v1/terminal/:session (WebSocket upgrade).
Add integration tests: create session, invalid worktree, session limit.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: This is the most complex backend task. Spec section 7.19: 'PTY over WebSocket. Session lifecycle: create/attach/detach/kill; idle cleanup. Directory restriction.' Consider using tokio-pty-process or portable-pty for cross-platform PTY support. WebSocket support requires axum's ws feature.

tasks.title: Enhance Dashboard with project summary, worktrees, and alerts
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The current Dashboard shows basic coordinator status and run/stop buttons. The spec (section 7.3) requires a much richer overview.
Key actions: Enhance the Dashboard with: project summary (path, branch, dirty state, MACC version), coordinator summary with KPI cards, worktrees summary, recent activity feed, and alerts section.
Out of scope: Config editing. Detailed task views.
Success criteria: Dashboard shows all five sections from the spec. KPI cards use the shared KpiCard component. Data is fetched from API endpoints. Auto-refreshes every 10s.
tasks.objective: Make the Dashboard the operator's primary overview screen.
tasks.result: Updated web/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx with rich project overview.
steps: Add project summary section: display project path, current branch, dirty state (from status API or new endpoint).
Enhance coordinator summary: use KpiCard components for total/todo/active/blocked/merged/paused counts.
Add worktrees summary section: call getWorktrees() API, show total/active/idle/stale counts.
Add recent activity feed: show last 5 coordinator events from the existing SSE stream or status endpoint.
Add alerts section: show doctor warnings, throttled tools, stale tasks (from status + doctor APIs).
Add auto-refresh (10s interval) using setInterval + Zustand store update.
Style with dark theme tokens and responsive grid layout.
tasks.notes: The existing Dashboard has coordinator status + run/stop buttons. Keep those and add the new sections around them. Use the shared KpiCard and StatusBadge components from WEB2-UI-002.

tasks.title: Build Tools & Adapters configuration page
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.4) requires a page to view and configure tool adapters with a card grid and right drawer editor.
Key actions: Create /config/tools page with adapter cards showing name, version, state, capabilities. Add a right drawer for editing tool settings. Include search and filter controls.
Out of scope: Tool installation workflow (just configuration). Install button can link to terminal.
Success criteria: Cards display all configured tools with their state. Clicking a card opens the right drawer with settings. Changes can be saved and trigger a config write via API. Filter by search/enabled/installed works.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based tool adapter configuration.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/config/Tools.tsx with card grid and right drawer editor.
steps: Create web/src/pages/config/Tools.tsx.
Fetch config via getConfig() API to get tool list and settings.
Render a card grid: each card shows tool name, version, enabled/disabled toggle, healthy/degraded badge, capabilities chips.
Add header actions: Check Updates button, filter bar (search, All/Enabled/Installed segmented control).
Implement right drawer (using RightDrawer component): show tool settings as form fields, raw view toggle (JSON).
Track unsaved changes in local state. Show apply/revert buttons in drawer.
On save: call updateConfig() API with the updated tool settings section.
Style with dark theme. Cards use consistent radius, border, shadow per spec.
tasks.notes: Spec section 7.4 details: 'Grid of adapter cards: name, version, category, capability chips. State: Enabled/Disabled + Healthy/Degraded + Idle/Active. Right drawer: schema-driven sections (container settings, mounts, network access, env, timeouts). Raw view toggle + copy. Unsaved changes handling.'

tasks.title: Build Standards configuration page
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.5) requires a Standards page to select presets and overrides.
Key actions: Create /config/standards page with preset selector, override editor, diff preview, and lint warnings.
Out of scope: Creating new presets.
Success criteria: Presets can be selected. Overrides are editable. Preview shows rendered output per tool. Diff from preset is visible. Lint warnings appear for inconsistencies.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based standards configuration.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/config/Standards.tsx with preset selection and override editing.
steps: Create web/src/pages/config/Standards.tsx.
Fetch config to get current standards settings.
Show preset selector dropdown with available presets.
Show override editor (key-value form fields) for conventions that differ from preset.
Show read-only preview of rendered output per tool (what each tool's config file would contain).
Show diff between current settings and selected preset (highlight overrides).
Display lint warnings if conventions are inconsistent.
On save: call updateConfig() with updated standards section.
tasks.notes: Standards are part of .macc/macc.yaml. The Engine handles preset resolution. The web page just reads/writes the standards section of the config.

tasks.title: Build Skills, Agents, and MCP catalog page
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.6) requires a catalog browser for skills, agents, and MCP servers with search, install flow, and security review.
Key actions: Create /config/skills page with catalog browser, search/filter controls, install flow with security review modal, and cache status.
Out of scope: Remote package execution. Only data-only installs.
Success criteria: Catalog items are browsable with search. Install flow shows permissions/risks in a Security Review modal. Configuration is editable. Cache status is visible.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based skill and MCP catalog management.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/config/Skills.tsx with catalog browser and install flow.
steps: Create web/src/pages/config/Skills.tsx.
Fetch config to get installed skills and MCP servers.
Implement catalog browser: list items with name, tool compatibility, verified status, source kind.
Add search bar and filter controls: by tool, by kind (skill/agent/MCP), by installed status.
Implement install flow: Add by URL button, Security Review modal showing permissions and risks (env, network, fs access), Configuration tab, Manifest tab (raw macc.package.json).
Show cache status per item: cached/not cached badge.
Add remove button for installed items (with confirmation).
On install/remove: call appropriate API endpoints (may need new endpoints or use config update).

tasks.title: Build Settings page (General, Coordinator, Advanced)
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 6.1) includes a Settings page for general, coordinator, and advanced configuration.
Key actions: Create /config/settings page with tabbed sections for General, Coordinator, and Advanced settings. Each tab shows editable form fields from macc.yaml.
Out of scope: Tool-specific settings (handled in Tools page).
Success criteria: All three tabs render with correct current values from config. Fields are editable with validation. Save writes to config via API. Coordinator settings include all automation.coordinator fields.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based general and coordinator settings editing.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/config/Settings.tsx with tabbed settings editor.
steps: Create web/src/pages/config/Settings.tsx.
Fetch config via getConfig() API.
Implement General tab: web_port, offline mode, quiet mode.

Implement Coordinator tab: max_dispatch, max_parallel, timeout_seconds, stale thresholds, error_code_retry_list, error_code_retry_max, rate_limit settings (backoff_base, backoff_max, fallback_enabled, throttle_parallel), tool_priority list.
Implement Advanced tab: raw YAML/JSON editor with syntax highlighting.
Track unsaved changes. Show save/discard buttons.
On save: validate and call updateConfig() API.
Show toast notification on success/failure.

tasks.title: Build PRD Editor with table view and detail pane
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.7) requires a PRD editor with virtualized table, task detail pane, and form editor.
Key actions: Create /prd page with a sortable/filterable table of tasks and a detail pane for editing individual tasks. Table must be virtualized for large PRDs.
Out of scope: Graph view and diff view (separate tasks).
Success criteria: Table shows all tasks with ID, title, state, priority, dependencies. Clicking a task opens detail pane with form editor. Changes can be saved with backup. Validation errors are shown before save.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based PRD task viewing and editing.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/Prd.tsx with virtualized table and detail pane.
steps: Create web/src/pages/Prd.tsx.
Install @tanstack/react-table and @tanstack/react-virtual for virtualized table.
Fetch PRD via getPrd() API.
Render virtualized table with columns: ID, title, category, priority, state, dependencies count, exclusive_resources count.
Add column sorting and text search filter.

Implement task detail pane (right drawer or split view): form fields for title, description, objective, result, steps (list editor), notes, priority (dropdown), dependencies (multi-select from task IDs), exclusive_resources (tag input).
Add raw JSON editor toggle in detail pane.
Track unsaved changes at PRD level. Show save/discard controls.
On save: validate (required fields, valid dependency IDs, no circular deps), call updatePrd() API.
Show validation errors inline and in a summary banner.
tasks.notes: Spec: 'Table view (virtualized) + filters/sorts. Task detail pane: form editor + raw JSON editor. Validation required to save (or explicit save anyway with warnings). Backups on save.' Use @tanstack/react-table for headless table with sorting/filtering.

tasks.title: Add PRD dependency graph view
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.7) requires a graph view showing the dependency DAG with cycle detection and critical path highlighting.
Key actions: Add a Graph tab/toggle to the PRD page that renders tasks as nodes and dependencies as edges. Detect cycles. Highlight the critical path.
Out of scope: Resource contention visualization (that's in the Locks page).
Success criteria: DAG renders with nodes for each task colored by state/priority. Edges show dependencies. Cycles are detected and highlighted with a warning banner. Zoom/pan/reset controls work. Clicking a node opens the task detail pane.
tasks.objective: Visualize PRD task dependencies for planning.
tasks.result: Graph view component added to web/src/pages/Prd.tsx.
steps: Install a graph rendering library (reactflow or d3-dag or elkjs).
Create PrdGraph component that converts PRD tasks into nodes and dependency edges.
Layout nodes using a DAG layout algorithm (top-to-bottom or left-to-right).
Color nodes by task state (todo=neutral, active=blue, merged=green, etc.).
Implement cycle detection: traverse the dependency graph, highlight cycles in red, show warning banner.
Implement critical path highlighting: find the longest dependency chain, highlight in bold.
Add zoom/pan/reset controls.
On node click: open the task detail pane (same as table row click).
Add toggle between Table and Graph views (persisted in local storage).
tasks.notes: reactflow is recommended as it provides built-in zoom/pan, node rendering, edge routing, and minimap. It works well with React and supports custom node components.

tasks.title: Add PRD diff view
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.7) requires a diff view comparing unsaved vs saved PRD and project PRD vs worktree PRD.
Key actions: Add a Diff tab to the PRD page that shows a unified diff between two versions of the PRD.
Out of scope: Merge tools. Just read-only diff display.
Success criteria: Unsaved vs saved diff shows pending changes. Project vs worktree diff shows divergence. Diff is displayed with syntax highlighting (additions green, deletions red). Diff is navigable by hunk.
tasks.objective: Enable PRD change review before saving.
tasks.result: Diff view component added to web/src/pages/Prd.tsx.
steps: Install a diff rendering library (react-diff-viewer-continued or similar).
Create PrdDiff component that accepts two JSON strings and renders a diff.
Implement 'Unsaved vs Saved' mode: compare current editor state with last saved version.
Implement 'Project vs Worktree' mode: fetch both project and worktree PRDs via API, compare.
Add a mode selector dropdown in the Diff tab.
Highlight additions in green, deletions in red, changes in yellow.
Add hunk navigation (previous/next change buttons).
Add toggle between unified and split diff views.

tasks.title: Build Plan page
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.8) requires a Plan page to run plan with options and display the ActionPlan with diffs and risk levels.
Key actions: Create /plan page with a form to configure plan options (scope, tools, offline flag) and a results area showing the ActionPlan.
Out of scope: Apply. Only plan preview.
Success criteria: User can configure plan scope and trigger a plan run. Results show file list with diffs, risk levels (safe/caution/dangerous color-coded), and consent requirements. Plan hash is displayed for reproducibility.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based plan preview.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/Plan.tsx with plan configuration and results display.

Add 'Run Plan' button that calls runPlan() API with the configured options.
Show loading state during plan execution.

Handle and display errors with the ErrorBanner component.

tasks.title: Build Apply page

Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.9) requires an Apply page with explicit confirmation, diff summary, and backup info.
Key actions: Create /apply page that shows the plan summary, requires confirmation, executes apply, and shows results.
Out of scope: User-scope writes.
Success criteria: Page shows plan summary with diffs and backup locations. Confirmation dialog (ConfirmDialog component) blocks apply until confirmed. Results show files written, backup location, and any warnings. Dry-run mode works.
tasks.objective: Enable web-based config apply with safety gates.
tasks.result: web/src/pages/Apply.tsx with confirmation flow and results display.

tasks.title: Build Worktrees page with table and card views

Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.10) requires a worktrees management page with two display modes (table and cards), KPI row, and bulk actions.

tasks.title: Build worktree create wizard
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 7.10) requires a multi-step wizard for creating worktrees.
Key actions: Create a wizard dialog/page with steps: basics (slug, tool, count), base branch + scope, review, create.

tasks.title: Implement git graph data API endpoint
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The web UI needs a git commit graph that is always visible (side panel or full page). The backend must provide commit data with parent references for graph rendering.
Key actions: Add GET /api/v1/git/graph endpoint that returns commit history with parent SHAs, branch refs, and MACC task ID annotations. Extend the existing git CLI wrapper in core/src/git.rs with a new function that includes parent hashes in the output format.
Out of scope: Diff content per commit. File change lists. Only graph topology and metadata.
Success criteria: GET returns an array of commits with sha, short_sha, subject, author, timestamp, parent_shas (array), branch_refs (array of branch names pointing to this commit), and optional task_id (extracted via the existing [macc:task] tag parser). Supports ?limit=N and ?since=SHA query params for pagination. At least 4 integration tests.
tasks.objective: Provide structured git graph data for the frontend commit graph visualization.
tasks.result: New git log function in core/src/git.rs. New endpoint in cli/src/commands/web/git.rs with tests.
steps: Add a new function to core/src/git.rs: git_log_graph(repo_path, limit, since_sha) that runs `git log --format='<NUL-delimited fields>' --parents --decorate=short` and parses output into a Vec<GitGraphCommit> struct with sha, parents, subject, author_name, author_date, refs.
Define GitGraphCommit struct in core/src/git.rs with fields: sha (String), parents (Vec<String>), subject (String), author (String), timestamp (i64), refs (Vec<String>).
Create cli/src/commands/web/git.rs.

Define API response types: ApiGitGraphResponse { commits: Vec<ApiGitCommit>, branches: Vec<String>, head: String }. ApiGitCommit maps from GitGraphCommit + optional task_id from [macc:task] tag parsing (reuse commit_reconciler logic).
Implement get_git_graph_handler: accept query params ?limit (default 100) and ?since (optional SHA for pagination). Call git_log_graph(), annotate with task IDs, return JSON.
Register route: GET /api/v1/git/graph.
Add integration tests: graph with commits, empty repo, pagination with limit, pagination with since.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: The existing core/src/git.rs uses CLI-based git invocations (no git2/gitoxide crate). Follow the same pattern. The existing commit_reconciler.rs already parses git log with NUL-delimited format — reuse that parsing approach but include parent SHAs. The --parents flag adds parent hashes to each log line.

Create a GitGraph component that fetches data from GET /api/v1/git/graph and renders an interactive commit graph. Integrate it as both a side panel (persistent, collapsible) and a full-page route (/ops/git).
Out of scope: Commit diff viewing. File change lists. Cherry-pick or rebase actions.
Success criteria: Graph renders commits as nodes with branch rail lines and merge edges. Branch names are labeled. MACC task IDs are shown as badges on linked commits. Clicking a commit shows details (SHA, message, author, date, task ID). Side panel mode is collapsible and persists across navigation. Full page mode shows a larger graph with more history. Infinite scroll loads older commits on demand. Auto-refresh picks up new commits every 30s.

Add getGitGraph(limit?, since?) client function to web/src/api/client.ts.
Create web/src/stores/gitGraphStore.ts (Zustand): commits array, branches, head, loadGraph(), loadMore() (pagination), isLoading.

Create web/src/components/GitGraphPanel.tsx: collapsible side panel (right side, 350px default width, resizable). Renders the commit-graph component with data from store. Shows branch legend. Add collapse/expand toggle button. Persist collapsed state in localStorage.
Create web/src/pages/ops/Git.tsx: full-page route wrapping the same graph component with more vertical space and additional controls.

It supports interactive graphs, infinite scroll/pagination, customizable node styling, and click handlers. No other maintained React git graph library exists — @gitgraph/react is archived (2021). The side panel approach keeps the graph visible during normal workflow; the full page gives more space when needed for branch analysis.

tasks.title: Add API contract tests for all new backend endpoints
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: New backend endpoints need integration tests verifying the API contract (request/response shapes, error codes, status codes).
Key actions: Add integration tests for each new endpoint module using the established WebTestEngine mock + tower::ServiceExt pattern.
Out of scope: Load testing. Only contract verification.
Success criteria: Each new endpoint has at least 2 tests (success + error case). Tests verify response shape matches API types. Tests verify error codes are domain-specific (not MACC-WEB-0000). All tests pass with cargo test.
tasks.objective: Verify API contract compliance for all new endpoints.
tasks.result: Integration tests in each backend module (config.rs, prd.rs, worktrees.rs, etc.).
steps: Add tests to config.rs: GET success, PUT success, PUT validation error.
Add tests to prd.rs: GET success, PUT success, PUT invalid JSON, GET missing PRD.
Add tests to plan.rs: POST success, POST with invalid scope.
Add tests to apply.rs: POST dry-run, POST confirmed, POST unconfirmed.
Add tests to worktrees.rs: GET list, POST create, DELETE success, DELETE not found.
Add tests to registry.rs: GET list, POST requeue, POST invalid action.
Add tests to logs.rs: GET list, GET read, GET path traversal rejection.
Add tests to doctor.rs: GET report, POST fix.
Add tests to backups.rs: GET list, POST restore.
Verify all error responses use domain-specific codes.
Run cargo test.
tasks.notes: Follow the existing test pattern in web.rs: build test app with WebTestEngine mock, send request via tower::ServiceExt::oneshot(), assert status code and response body. At least 20 new tests total across all modules.

tasks.title: Add streaming reliability tests
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 12) requires streaming reliability tests for SSE reconnect, backoff, and concurrent streams.
Key actions: Add backend tests for SSE endpoint behavior (heartbeat, Last-Event-ID replay, concurrent connections). Add frontend tests for useEventSource reconnect behavior.
Out of scope: Network simulation. Just logical behavior tests.
Success criteria: Backend tests verify: heartbeat interval, Last-Event-ID replay, max concurrent streams. Frontend tests verify: reconnect with backoff, gap detection, event buffering. All tests pass.
tasks.objective: Verify streaming reliability under edge conditions.
tasks.result: Backend SSE tests and frontend useEventSource tests.
steps: Backend: test heartbeat is sent within expected interval.
Backend: test Last-Event-ID header triggers replay from correct position.
Backend: test concurrent SSE connections up to limit.
Backend: test graceful handling when log file is rotated.
Frontend: test useEventSource reconnects after disconnect.
Frontend: test exponential backoff increases delay between reconnects.
Frontend: test gap detection when Last-Event-ID response indicates missed events.
Frontend: test event buffer limits (oldest events truncated).
tasks.notes: Use the existing SSE test patterns from the coordinator events endpoint. For frontend tests, mock EventSource behavior to simulate disconnect/reconnect scenarios.

tasks.title: Add virtualized tables and lazy loading
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 10) requires virtualized tables for large PRDs and logs, and initial load < 2s.
Key actions: Apply virtualization to PRD table, registry table, and log content display. Add route-based code splitting via React.lazy (already planned in UI-004). Optimize bundle size.
Out of scope: Server-side pagination (handled by API endpoints already).
Success criteria: PRD table handles 500+ tasks without performance degradation. Log viewer handles 10000+ lines smoothly. Initial bundle loads in < 2s (measure with Lighthouse). No jank during scrolling.
tasks.objective: Ensure UI performance at scale.
tasks.result: Virtualized table components and optimized bundle splitting.
steps: Verify @tanstack/react-virtual is installed and used in PRD table.
Apply virtualization to registry task table.
Apply virtualization to log content display (virtual scroll for log lines).
Verify React.lazy code splitting is working for all routes (check bundle analyzer).
Add loading skeletons for lazy-loaded pages.
Audit and tree-shake unused dependencies.
Run Lighthouse performance audit on key pages (dashboard, console, PRD editor).
Fix any performance issues identified.
tasks.notes: Spec section 10: 'Virtualized tables for large PRD and large logs. UI initial load < 2s on medium projects.' @tanstack/react-virtual is the recommended virtualization library for React.

tasks.title: Accessibility audit and fixes
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: The spec (section 11) requires full keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, color-agnostic indicators, and reduced motion support.
Key actions: Audit all pages for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Fix identified issues. Add skip-to-content links, focus management, and reduced motion CSS.
Out of scope: Screen reader optimization beyond ARIA basics.
Success criteria: All interactive elements are keyboard accessible. Status indicators use icon + text (not color only). prefers-reduced-motion is respected. No critical axe-core violations.
tasks.objective: Ensure the web UI is accessible to all operators.
tasks.result: Accessibility fixes across all pages. prefers-reduced-motion media query in CSS.
steps: Install axe-core or similar accessibility testing tool.
Run automated audit on all pages.
Fix critical violations: missing ARIA labels, inaccessible buttons, keyboard traps.
Add skip-to-content link at top of layout.
Verify all status indicators use icon + text label (never color only).
Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) CSS to disable animations.
Verify tab order is logical on all pages.
Test keyboard-only navigation through key workflows (dashboard → console → run coordinator → view logs).
tasks.notes: Spec section 11: 'Full keyboard navigation, proper ARIA labels, color-agnostic status indicators (icons + text), reduced motion support.' StatusBadge component (WEB2-UI-002) should already include icon + text — verify this.

tasks.title: Update web UI documentation
tasks.description:
Problem/Goal: Documentation needs to cover all new features, API endpoints, and development workflows added in this lot.
Key actions: Update README.md Web UI section, update or create docs/WEB_API_CONTRACT.md with all new endpoints, update CHANGELOG.md.
Out of scope: User-facing tutorial content.
Success criteria: README covers all dev and production workflows. API contract doc lists all endpoints with request/response shapes. CHANGELOG has entries for all new features.
tasks.objective: Keep documentation current with the implementation.
tasks.result: Updated README.md, docs/WEB_API_CONTRACT.md, CHANGELOG.md.
steps: Update README.md Web UI section: add descriptions of all new pages and features.
Update docs/WEB_API_CONTRACT.md: add all new endpoints (config, PRD, plan, apply, worktrees, registry, logs, doctor, backups, terminal) with request/response examples.

tasks.notes: Documentation should be accurate and concise. Focus on what a developer needs to know to contribute or operate the web UI.

recommendations: Split web.rs into modules FIRST (WEB2-ARCH-001) to unblock all parallel backend work.
Use contracts-first approach: define all API types (WEB2-ARCH-002/003) before implementing handlers or pages.
Backend endpoint tasks (WEB2-BE-*) and frontend foundation tasks (WEB2-UI-*) can run in parallel once ARCH-001 is done.
Frontend page tasks can run in parallel as long as they don't share exclusive resources.
Terminal gateway (WEB2-BE-TERM-001) is the most complex backend task — schedule it for an experienced agent.
Prioritize the Coordinator Console (WEB2-FE-CONSOLE-001) and Registry (WEB2-FE-REG-001) as they provide the most operator value.

Tool Cooldown management (WEB2-BE-COOL-001 + WEB2-FE-COOL-001) is high priority — E602 quota exhaustion is a frequent event during coordinator runs. The backend endpoints are thin wrappers around existing SqliteStorage methods, making WEB2-BE-COOL-001 a quick win.
PRD Editor (WEB2-FE-PRD-001) is high-value for the MACC workflow and should be prioritized.
Use the existing test infrastructure (WebTestEngine, tower::ServiceExt) for all new backend tests.
No authentication: all endpoints are open. The web server binds to localhost by default for security.
Keep the SSE streaming pattern from the existing coordinator events endpoint when adding log tail streams.

Git Graph (WEB2-BE-GIT-001 + WEB2-FE-GIT-001) should use the `commit-graph` npm package — the only actively maintained React git graph library. Backend reuses the existing CLI-based git wrapper pattern in core/src/git.rs (no new Rust crate needed, just add --parents to git log). Prefer existing libraries over custom implementations throughout this lot.
